content: The Higgs boson itself is a fundamental particle that is essential for our understanding of the universe, as it helps explain how other particles acquire mass. Its discovery at the Large Hadron Collider has provided significant insights into particle physics and the Standard Model. While some may have concerns about the safety of high-energy physics experiments, there is currently no evidence to suggest that the Higgs boson or the experiments studying it pose any threat to the universe.
